title: Highly Desireable
content:
The boarder in the bar-room rose,
A pale gaunt man who lodged with Hann,
ï¿½I bear,ï¿½ he said, ï¿½the worst of woes,
And suffer torments no one knows,
For do my best I never can
Have sleep like any other man.

ï¿½I have insomnia,ï¿½ said he.
ï¿½At times it drives me mad outright.
Whateï¿½er I do, whereï¿½er I be,
Its just the same, so sleep for me.
You wonï¿½t believe for three years quite
I havenï¿½t slept two hours a night.ï¿½

Boss-cocky Billson softly swore,
And turning from his chestnut cob.
ï¿½Whatï¿½s that?ï¿½ he questioned from the door.
ï¿½You say that you donï¿½t sleep no more
Than two hours? I pay thirty bob.
Now, mister, do you want a job?ï¿½
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Dyson, titled "Highly Desireable", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
